    <h1 {...{
      "id": "add-multiple-panels"
    }}>{`Add Multiple Panels`}</h1>
    <p>{`Learn how to create a plugin with multiple panel entrypoints and control their display programmatically.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`While many plugins only need a single panel, UXP allows you to create `}<strong parentName="p">{`multiple panels within the same plugin`}</strong>{`. This is useful when you want to organize different features into separate, focused interfaces—for example, a main control panel and a separate settings or preview panel. You'll learn how to:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Declare multiple panel entrypoints in your manifest`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Structure your HTML to support multiple panels`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Use lifecycle hooks to control panel visibility`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Open panels programmatically from other panels`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`This tutorial builds on concepts from the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../concepts/entrypoints/index.md"
      }}>{`Entrypoints`}</a>{` and `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../add-lifecycle-hooks/index.md"
      }}>{`Lifecycle Hooks`}</a>{` guides. If you're new to these topics, we recommend reviewing those first. To programmatically open a panel from another panel, you'll need to use the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../inter-plugin-comm/index.md"
      }}>{`Inter-Plugin Communication`}</a>{` tutorial, but it's not strictly necessary to understand how to create multi-panel plugins.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "key-concepts"
    }}>{`Key Concepts`}</h2>
    <p>{`A single plugin can expose multiple panels, each appearing as a separate menu item under `}<strong parentName="p">{`Window`}</strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p">{`UXP Plugins`}</strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p">{`ThePluginName`}</strong>{`. Each panel:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Has its own entrypoint definition in the manifest`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Can have its own size constraints and configuration`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Shares the same HTML document and JavaScript context`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Can communicate with other panels in the same plugin`}</li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`IPC Permission`}</p>
    <p>{`To enable inter-panel communication and programmatic panel control, you need to enable the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ipc`}</inlineCode>{` (Inter-Process Communication) permission in your manifest.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  // ...
  "requiredPermissions": {
    "ipc": { "enablePluginCommunication": true }
  }
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`When working with multiple panels, you'll use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`show()`}</inlineCode>{` `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../concepts/entrypoints/index.md#panel-lifecycle-hooks"
      }}>{`lifecycle hook`}</a>{` to append the appropriate content to the panel when it's displayed. This approach keeps your panel content organized and ensures each panel only displays its relevant UI.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="header, text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Known Limitation`}</p>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{` lifecycle hook is `}<strong parentName="p">{`not currently working as expected`}</strong>{` in Premiere. This means that when a panel is hidden, the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{` callback won't be reliably triggered. For now, focus on using the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`show()`}</inlineCode>{` hook to append content, and be aware that DOM cleanup in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{` may not execute. This will be fixed in a future update.`}</p>

    <h3 {...{
      "id": "1-declare-multiple-entrypoints"
    }}>{`1. Declare Multiple Entrypoints`}</h3>
    <p>{`First, update your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` to declare two panel entrypoints. Notice that we also enable the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ipc`}</inlineCode>{` permission:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "id": "multi-panel-demo",
  "name": "Multi Panel Demo",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "main": "index.html",
  "host": { "app": "premierepro", "minVersion": "25.6.0" },
  "manifestVersion": 5,
  "requiredPermissions": {
    // 👇 Required for inter-panel control
    "ipc": { "enablePluginCommunication": true }
  },
  "entrypoints": [
    {
      "id": "uxp-first-panel",
      "type": "panel",
      "label": { "default": "First Panel" },
      "minimumSize": { "width": 430, "height": 500 },
      "preferredDockedSize": { "width": 230, "height": 300 }
      // ...
    },
    {
      "id": "uxp-second-panel",
      "type": "panel",
      "label": { "default": "Second Panel" },
      "minimumSize": { "width": 430, "height": 500 },
      "preferredDockedSize": { "width": 230, "height": 300 }
      // ...
    }
  ]
  // ...
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Both panels will now appear in the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Window`}</strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p">{`UXP Plugins`}</strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p">{`Multi Panel Demo`}</strong>{` menu.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "2-structure-your-html"
    }}>{`2. Structure Your HTML`}</h3>
    <p>{`In your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`index.html`}</inlineCode>{`, create separate wrapper containers for each panel. The second panel needs an `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id`}</inlineCode>{` attribute so we can reference it in JavaScript later.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-html"
      }}>{`<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
  <script src="main.js"></script>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css" />
</head>
<body>
  <!-- First panel content -->
  <div class="wrapper">
    <sp-heading>First Panel</sp-heading>
    <sp-divider size="L"></sp-divider>
    <sp-body>
      This is the first panel.
    </sp-body>
    <sp-button id="open-second-panel">Open Second Panel</sp-button>
  </div>

  <!-- Second panel content -->
  <div class="wrapper" id="second-panel">
    <sp-heading>Second Panel</sp-heading>
    <sp-divider size="L"></sp-divider>
    <sp-body>
      This is the second panel.
    </sp-body>
  </div>
</body>
</html>
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Important notes:`}</strong></p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Both panels share the same HTML document.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Only the second `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`<div>`}</inlineCode>{` wrapper needs an `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`id`}</inlineCode>{` because we'll append it to the second panel's body and we need a means to reference it.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The first panel's content is already in the DOM and doesn't need special handling.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<div>`}</inlineCode>{` wrapper `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id`}</inlineCode>{` (holding the second panel's content) can be different from the second panel's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id`}</inlineCode>{` in the manifest.json.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "3-implement-lifecycle-hooks"
    }}>{`3. Implement Lifecycle Hooks`}</h3>
    <p>{`Now, let's use lifecycle hooks to programmatically append the second panel's content to the DOM when the second panel is shown. In your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`main.js`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const { entrypoints, pluginManager } = require("uxp");

// Get reference to the second panel container
const secondPanel = document.querySelector("#second-panel");
let PLUGIN_ID;

entrypoints.setup({
  plugin: {
    create() {
      // Store the plugin ID for later use
      // (IPC -> opening the second panel programmatically)
      PLUGIN_ID = this.id;
      console.log("Plugin created:", PLUGIN_ID);
    },
  },
  panels: {
    "uxp-first-panel": {
      create() { console.log("First panel created") },
      show() {
        console.log("First panel shown");
        // The first panel content is already in the DOM
        // no other action is needed.
      },
    },
    "uxp-second-panel": {
      create() { console.log("Second panel created") },
      show(body) {
        // Append the second panel content when this panel is shown
        body.appendChild(secondPanel); // 👈 Key: append on show
        console.log("Second panel shown");
      },
      hide(body) {
        // ⚠️ Note: This hook currently doesn't work reliably
        body.removeChild(secondPanel);
        console.log("Second panel hidden");
      },
    },
  },
});

// Add button handler to open the second panel via IPC
document.querySelector("#open-second-panel")
  .addEventListener("click", () => {
    // Find this plugin in the list of all plugins
    const me = [...pluginManager.plugins].find(
      (plugin) => plugin.id === PLUGIN_ID
    );
    // Open the second panel programmatically
    me?.showPanel("uxp-second-panel"); // 👈 Opens the second panel
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "how-it-works"
    }}>{`How It Works`}</h2>
    <p>{`Let's break down the key mechanisms:`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "1-shared-html-context"
    }}>{`1. Shared HTML Context`}</h3>
    <p>{`Both panels share the same `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`index.html`}</inlineCode>{` document. When you open either panel from the `}<strong parentName="p">{`Window`}</strong>{` menu, Premiere displays the same HTML document—but which content is visible depends on the panel's lifecycle hooks.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "2-dynamic-content-appending"
    }}>{`2. Dynamic Content Appending`}</h3>
    <p>{`The `}<strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`show()`}</inlineCode>{` lifecycle hook`}</strong>{` receives the panel's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`body`}</inlineCode>{` (root node) as a parameter. When the second panel is shown, we use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`body.appendChild(secondPanel)`}</inlineCode>{` to inject its content into the DOM. This keeps the second panel's content hidden until explicitly displayed.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "3-programmatic-panel-control"
    }}>{`3. Programmatic Panel Control`}</h3>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`pluginManager`}</inlineCode>{` object provides access to all loaded plugins—see a dedicated tutorial `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../inter-plugin-comm/index.md"
      }}>{`here`}</a>{`. By finding our own plugin in the list (using the stored `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`PLUGIN_ID`}</inlineCode>{`), we can call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`showPanel()`}</inlineCode>{` to open the second panel programmatically:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const me = [...pluginManager.plugins].find(
  (plugin) => plugin.id === PLUGIN_ID
);
me?.showPanel("uxp-second-panel"); // Opens the panel by entrypoint ID
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
You can **open panels programmatically, but you cannot close them** via the API. Users must close panels manually through the Premiere interface.
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "4-inter-panel-communication"
    }}>{`4. Inter-Panel Communication`}</h3>
    <p>{`Since both panels share the same JavaScript context, they can easily communicate by sharing variables, calling functions, and using events.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "use-multiple-html-files"

    <p>{`You can also use `}<strong parentName="p">{`multiple HTML files, one for each panel`}</strong>{`. This is useful when you want to keep the code for each panel separate and avoid clutter in the main `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`index.html`}</inlineCode>{` file. The key is to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fetch()`}</inlineCode>{` the second panel's HTML file and inject the content into the DOM, as the following example shows.`}</p>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "summary"
    }}>{`Summary`}</h2>
    <p>{`Multiple panels enable you to organize complex plugins into focused, manageable interfaces that users can access independently.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Key Takeaways:`}</strong></p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Declare multiple entrypoints`}</strong>{` in `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` with `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`type: "panel"`}</inlineCode>{` for each panel.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Structure the HTML with wrappers`}</strong>{`: Use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`id`}</inlineCode>{` attributes for panels that need dynamic control.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Use the `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`show()`}</inlineCode>{` hook`}</strong>{` to append panel content to the DOM when the panel is displayed. Ideally, use `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{` to remove the content when the panel is hidden.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol"><strong parentName="li">{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`pluginManager.showPanel()`}</inlineCode></strong>{` to open one panel from another programmatically (remember to enable IPC permissions). Panels cannot be closed programmatically, only opened.`}</li>
    </ol>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
;
MDXContent.isMDXComponent = true;
